summary: First round of end-to-end testing for the Meeting Room Booking MVP (FastAPI + React + MongoDB). Ran 22 backend pytest cases against the public URL and a full Playwright UI flow covering login, dashboard, booking creation, validation, calendar (week/month), room filter, My Bookings, Admin (overview, rooms maintenance toggle, users, all bookings), and logout. All 22 backend tests passed (auth cookies, room status, booking validation/overlap/maintenance, mine filter, edit-forbidden, soft cancel, admin RBAC, analytics shape). All frontend flows worked end-to-end with no critical issues.

minor.issue: set-cookie uses secure=False and samesite=lax with HTTP. Works behind the current preview proxy, but in production over a stricter cross-origin context this should be secure=True + samesite=None.

backend/.env has CORS_ORIGINS="*" but server.py ignores it and uses FRONTEND_URL+http://localhost:3000. The CORS_ORIGINS env var is effectively dead code – either wire it up or remove it from .env to avoid confusion.

test_credentials.md documents DELETE /api/admin/bookings/{id} but no such route exists. Admin cancels via the regular DELETE /api/bookings/{id} (allowed by role check). Either add the documented route or update the docs.

issues: A toast appears in the top-right corner on the public /login route from a prior session (e.g., 'Meeting Room 1 -> Active') because the toaster mounts before logout cleans state. Cosmetic only.

action_items: Optional: drop unused CORS_ORIGINS="*" from backend/.env or wire it into server.py CORSMiddleware.
Optional: align docs (test_credentials.md) with the actual admin cancel route (it uses DELETE /api/bookings/{id}).
Production hardening: set cookies with secure=True and samesite='none' when serving over HTTPS in production.
critical_code_review_comments: Cookies are set with secure=False even though the deployment is HTTPS-only. Safe for preview but should be flipped in production.

server.py is ~700 lines and starting to mix concerns (models, helpers, auth, routes, seeding). Split into routers/auth.py, routers/rooms.py, routers/bookings.py, routers/admin.py, seed.py once it grows further.
validate_booking_times rejects bookings >12h but doesn't enforce minimum duration. Consider a 5-min minimum.
rooms_status currently iterates rooms and does an awaited DB call per room (N+1). For 3 rooms this is fine; if you ever scale, do a single $in query.
admin_analytics uses datetime.fromisoformat on stored ISO strings - works because we store with tz suffix, but worth documenting that all stored datetimes are UTC ISO.
Frontend BookingDialog builds ISO from local date/time inputs via the browser's Date constructor; daylight-saving edges are handled by the browser, which is acceptable for an internal tool.
test_credentials: Admin admin@company.com / admin123; Employees jane|alex|priya|sam @company.com / employee123 (from /app/memory/test_credentials.md)
seed_data_creation: Created and cleaned up TEST_ prefixed bookings during the run. Backend auto-seeds 1 admin + 4 employees + 6 sample bookings on startup; left untouched.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest suite lives at /app/backend/tests/test_meeting_rooms.py and is fully green. Re-run with: pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml. Public URL: https://space-reserve-22.preview.emergentagent.com. Frontend data-testids are comprehensive (login-*, dashboard-root, rooms-grid, quick-book-btn, booking-*, nav-*, calendar-*, view-*, tl-*, month-day-*, admin-*, maintenance-toggle-*, logout-button). Boardroom is seeded with an active booking spanning now-30m..now+90m, so 'occupied' state is testable.
